summary: A modern Asian-inspired pavilion structure featuring four tall wooden pillars with tiered stone roofs. The building has a central open courtyard with a rectangular footprint, surrounded by a stone foundation and decorative stone pathway. The design emphasizes vertical elements with stacked roof overhangs and minimal enclosed walls, creating an open-air gathering space.

shape.overall_form: rectangular open-air pavilion with four corner towers

rebuild_hints: Build four tall wooden pillars at corners, approximately 10 blocks tall
Stack stone slabs in tiered layers above each pillar to create pagoda-style roofs
Create a rectangular stone foundation around the entire structure
Lay decorative stone brick pathway connecting all four corners
Add wooden wall panels between pillars with vertical grain pattern
Place lanterns on top of each roof tier for detail
Leave center courtyard completely open with dirt/sand flooring
Ensure bilateral symmetry across both north-south and east-west axes
Use dark wood for pillars and lighter stone for roof contrast
uncertainties: Exact block count for pillar height uncertain from angles shown
Specific stone brick variant (normal vs mossy) not clearly distinguishable
Interior courtyard flooring material appears to be dirt or sand - exact type unclear
Roof overhang depth varies by angle; exact measurement uncertain
Wall panel interior details not fully visible from exterior views
